mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 3.5, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production and decline in inflation signals robust supply-side growth and stable pricing environments, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and CPIAUCSL fails to explain more than 5% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or the combined signal produces negative returns in 40%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly expansion in GDP combined with rising housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1-HOUST signal fails to explain more than 8% of SPX return variance in rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of cases with positive signals.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2% signals monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2.0, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns exceeds 0.15 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in payrolls and decline in unemployment signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate profits and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain more than 3% of SPX return variance in out-of-sample tests, or 5-day returns are negative in 40%+ of cases with positive signals.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real sales of durable goods indicates strong consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS rises by more than 1% month-over-month, or the correlation between RSAFS and SPX returns falls below 0.15 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply-side activity that reduces corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO < 108.5, or the correlation between INDPRO and SPX returns exceeds -0.1 in rolling 1-year windows.
